root_cause: Abrasive particles in process fluid caused accelerated seal face wear. Strainer upstream of pump was bypassed during maintenance and not reinstated.
immediate_cause: Seal faces worn beyond allowable limits causing product leakage
contributing_factors: Strainer bypass not closed after maintenance
No seal flush plan implemented
Maintenance procedure not followed
actions_taken: Replaced mechanical seal with upgraded API Plan 11 flush. Installed strainer and interlocked bypass valve. Revised maintenance procedure.
lessons_learned: Always verify strainer is in service before starting pump. Mechanical seals on crude service pumps must have flush plan. Checklist added to startup procedure.

root_cause: Suction valve plate cracked due to liquid carryover from upstream separator. Separator high-level alarm not responded to promptly.
immediate_cause: Liquid slug entered compressor cylinder causing valve plate fracture
contributing_factors: High level in separator — alarm not actioned within 15 minutes
Operator on break without proper handover
No automatic compressor trip on high separator level
actions_taken: Replaced all suction valve plates. Added automatic compressor trip on separator high-high level. Revised alarm response procedure.
lessons_learned: Compressors must trip automatically on upstream separator high-high level. Alarm response time must be under 5 minutes. Liquid knockout critical for compressor protection.

root_cause: Under-deposit corrosion on shell side due to ammonium bisulphide accumulation. Last inspection 28 months ago — overdue per OISD-113.
immediate_cause: Tube wall thinned to below minimum thickness causing through-wall leak
contributing_factors: Inspection overdue by 4 months
No online corrosion monitoring
High NH4HS concentration in service
actions_taken: Plugged 14 tubes. Conducted full tube thickness survey. Established online UT monitoring patch. Revised inspection interval to 18 months.
lessons_learned: Heat exchangers in ammonium bisulphide service must be inspected every 18 months. Install injection quill for neutraliser upstream. Monitor velocity to keep below 3 m/s.

root_cause: Feedwater control valve FV-701 failed closed due to positioner failure. BFW pump auto-start circuit did not function — relay coil burned.
immediate_cause: Boiler drum level fell below trip setpoint triggering automatic shutdown
contributing_factors: FV-701 positioner last calibrated 14 months ago — overdue
Standby BFW pump auto-start never tested in 2021
Night shift had only 1 operator for utilities section
actions_taken: Replaced FV-701 positioner. Repaired auto-start relay. Revised boiler operating procedure. Monthly auto-start test added to PM schedule.
lessons_learned: BFW pump auto-start must be tested monthly. Feedwater control valve positioners are critical — calibrate every 6 months. Never operate boiler section with single operator on night shift.

root_cause: Thrust bearing failure due to oil contamination. Lube oil cooler was bypassed during winter startup — condensate entered lube oil system.
immediate_cause: Bearing overheated and seized causing pump trip on high vibration
contributing_factors: Lube oil cooler bypass procedure not documented
No lube oil quality check done at startup
Vibration alarm setpoint too high — should have triggered earlier
actions_taken: Replaced thrust bearing. Flushed lube oil system. Installed lube oil water-in-oil sensor. Added startup checklist item for oil quality.
lessons_learned: Lube oil cooler must never be bypassed without oil quality verification. Check lube oil water content before any startup. Lower vibration trip setpoint from 12mm/s to 8mm/s for reciprocating pumps.

root_cause: Hydrogen induced cracking (HIC) found in shell of V-103 during shutdown inspection. Hardness values above NACE MR0175 limits on weld HAZ.
immediate_cause: Material was non-compliant with HIC service requirements
contributing_factors: Original construction specification did not mandate HIC-resistant steel
No baseline hardness survey done at commissioning
Inspection focused only on corrosion — HIC not on scope
actions_taken: Repair welded per ASME Section IX. Post-weld heat treatment done. HIC-resistant cladding applied to affected areas. Inspection scope updated to include UT phased array for all HDS vessels.
lessons_learned: All vessels in hydrogen service must be inspected for HIC using phased array UT. Weld hardness must meet NACE MR0175 limits. Establish baseline hardness map at commissioning.

root_cause: Radiant section tube skin temperature exceeded design limit due to maldistribution of flow. One pass flow indicator was reading incorrectly — orifice plate damaged.
immediate_cause: Pass flow imbalance caused one set of tubes to run dry at reduced flow
contributing_factors: Damaged orifice plate not detected during previous calibration
No tube skin temperature monitoring on overheated pass
Operator increased firing rate without checking individual pass flows
actions_taken: Replaced 6 overheated tubes. Replaced orifice plate. Installed thermocouple on all radiant passes. Added tube skin temperature high alarm to DCS.
lessons_learned: Install tube skin temperature monitoring on all radiant passes. Never increase firing rate without verifying individual pass flows. Orifice plates must be pulled and inspected every 5 years.

root_cause: SRV simmering continuously due to set pressure drift downward. Valve was 16 months overdue for testing and recertification.
immediate_cause: Set pressure had drifted from 15.5 bar to 13.2 bar causing continuous simmering at normal operating pressure of 13 bar
contributing_factors: SRV testing schedule not maintained — missed 12-month recertification
No monitoring of SRV tailpipe temperature to detect simmering
Spare SRV not available for hot swap
actions_taken: SRV removed, bench-tested and recertified. Spare SRV procured and kept ready. Monthly tailpipe temperature check added to operator round.
lessons_learned: SRV testing must never be deferred beyond 12 months on OISD-142 compliance. Monitor tailpipe temperature monthly — any warmth indicates simmering. Always maintain certified spare SRV.

root_cause: Impeller severely worn due to cavitation caused by low suction pressure during tank level drawdown. NPSH margin fell below 0.5m.
immediate_cause: Cavitation caused pitting erosion of impeller vanes reducing pump efficiency and eventually causing trip
contributing_factors: No low suction pressure alarm implemented
Tank level allowed to fall below minimum operating level
No NPSH monitoring during low level operation
actions_taken: Replaced impeller. Added low suction pressure alarm at 0.8 bar. Added pump auto-trip on low suction pressure. Established minimum tank level SOP.
lessons_learned: P-101 on crude service: always maintain tank level above minimum. Low suction pressure = high cavitation risk. Monitor vibration spike as early cavitation indicator.

root_cause: Downcomer flooding caused by high feed rate above design capacity. Liquid buildup collapsed 3 valve trays in the upper section.
immediate_cause: Feed rate pushed 15% above design — tray pressure drop doubled, causing flooding cascade
contributing_factors: Marketing pressure to maximize throughput
No high feed rate interlock
Tray inspection at last turnaround found minor issues — not repaired citing schedule pressure
actions_taken: Repaired and replaced damaged trays. Installed feed rate high-high interlock. Operations procedure updated with maximum throughput limits.
lessons_learned: Column tray damage noted at turnaround must be repaired immediately — not deferred. Feed rate interlocks are non-negotiable. Throughput beyond design capacity must be approved by process engineer.

root_cause: Discharge valve plate failure due to fatigue. Valve was 14 months old — design life 12 months. PM due date was missed during shutdown rescheduling.
immediate_cause: Fractured valve plate entered cylinder causing rod packing damage
contributing_factors: PM missed due to compressed shutdown schedule
No vibration trend analysis — early warning missed
No valve plate life tracking system
actions_taken: Replaced all valve plates. Replaced rod packing. Established valve plate life tracking in CMMS. Added monthly vibration trend analysis.
lessons_learned: Compressor valve plate replacement must not be deferred beyond design life. Monitor suction/discharge temperature differential as valve condition indicator. Track individual component lives in CMMS.

root_cause: Premature catalyst deactivation due to feed sulphur spike exceeding catalyst specification. Upstream crude blend changed without process review.
immediate_cause: H2S breakthrough detected downstream indicating catalyst saturation
contributing_factors: Crude blend change not routed through Management of Change process
No online feed analyser — sulfur monitored only by daily lab sample
No high sulphur feed alarm implemented
actions_taken: Regenerated catalyst. Installed online H2S analyser on feed. Crude blend change added to MoC scope. Daily sulphur limit added to feed specification.
lessons_learned: Any crude blend change is a Management of Change — must be reviewed by Process Engineering. Online feed analysis is critical for catalyst protection in HDS units. Define feed quality limits in unit operating envelope.

root_cause: Secondary O-ring on mechanical seal degraded due to prolonged exposure to crude oil aromatics above 160°C. Wrong elastomer grade (NBR instead of Viton) used during last seal replacement.
immediate_cause: O-ring failure caused seal gland leakage — crude weeping at shaft
contributing_factors: Wrong O-ring material selected from spare parts store — incorrect part number on work order
Inspection of seal material not part of pre-installation checklist
Viton O-rings not maintained as critical spare
actions_taken: Replaced seal with correct Viton O-ring. Updated spare parts specification. Added O-ring material verification to seal installation checklist.
lessons_learned: For crude service above 150°C: ALWAYS use Viton (FKM) elastomers — not NBR. Verify O-ring material grade against work order before installation. This is the third seal failure on P-101 — investigate root cause beyond elastomers.

root_cause: Severe fouling on shell side of E-101 (crude preheat service) reduced thermal efficiency by 35%. LMTD drop indicated fouling factor exceeded 0.0003 m²K/W. Cleaning overdue by 6 months.
immediate_cause: Product draw temperature from CDU dropped below specification causing quality issues
contributing_factors: Fouling rate higher than design due to paraffinic crude blend
Cleaning schedule not adjusted for new crude blend
No online fouling monitoring installed
actions_taken: High pressure water jet cleaning of shell side. Installed dP across bundle for fouling monitoring. Reduced cleaning interval from 18 to 12 months.
lessons_learned: Monitor dP across heat exchanger bundles as primary fouling indicator. Adjust cleaning frequency when crude blend changes. Do not wait for product quality deviation — act on dP trend.

root_cause: Control valve CV-102 developed severe hysteresis (±8%) due to sticky packing. Valve was oscillating causing process pressure cycling.
immediate_cause: Gland packing was overtightened during a routine check causing stem stiction
contributing_factors: Maintenance procedure for packing adjustment not followed — overtightened by approximately 2 turns
No post-maintenance valve stroke test required by procedure
Control loop in auto with tight tuning — amplified oscillation
actions_taken: Adjusted packing to correct torque. Stroked valve and verified hysteresis < 1%. Updated maintenance procedure with torque specification.
lessons_learned: Packing adjustment must follow torque specification — not feel. Stroke test after any valve maintenance before returning to service. Consider positioner with diagnostics on critical valves.
